Container.Name: Mediastarr
Container.Overview:
Automated missing-content and quality-upgrade search for Sonarr and Radarr.
Features: - Multiple Sonarr/Radarr instances with custom names (HD, 4K, Anime …) - Only Sonarr, only Radarr, or any mix — fully optional - SQLite search history with per-item cooldown (configurable in days) - Configurable daily search limit - Random jitter on hunt intervals (minimum 15 minutes enforced) - Discord webhook notifications with per-event toggles and rate-limit protection - Periodic statistics report to Discord - Sonarr search granularity: episode / season / full series - Upgrade search on/off toggle - Dark / Light / OLED themes - German and English UI + logs - First-run browser wizard — no config file editing required
Independent project, not affiliated with Huntarr.
Container.Requires: At least one running Sonarr or Radarr instance reachable from the container. Recommended: same Docker network as your Arr stack.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the Mediastarr web interface.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Stores config.json and the SQLite search history database. Must be a persistent path — without this, the setup wizard restarts on every container restart.

Config.Name: Sonarr API Key
Config.Description: Optional: Sonarr API key for automatic first-run setup. Find it in Sonarr → Settings → General → Security → API Key. If both SONARR_API_KEY and RADARR_API_KEY are set, the wizard is skipped.

Config.Name: Sonarr URL
Config.Description: Optional: Sonarr URL (used only with SONARR_API_KEY).

Config.Name: Radarr API Key
Config.Description: Optional: Radarr API key for automatic first-run setup. Find it in Radarr → Settings → General → Security → API Key.

Config.Name: Radarr URL
Config.Description: Optional: Radarr URL (used only with RADARR_API_KEY).

Config.Name: Data Directory (env)
Config.Description: Internal container path for the data directory. Change only if you know what you are doing.
